channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump repeats threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges if deal isn't reached
item.description: President Trump has repeated threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure if a deal isn't reached by Tuesday night.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has repeated threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure if a deal isn't reached by Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Rep. Adam Smith, D-Wash., talks about Trump's threats against Iran's infrastructure
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Rep. Adam Smith, the top Democrat on the House Armed Services Committee, about President Trump's continued threats against Iran and prospects for a ceasefire.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Rep. Adam Smith, the top Democrat on the House Armed Services Committee, about President Trump's continued threats against Iran and prospects for a ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump's deadline looms as strikes in the Middle East intensify
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse
item.description: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump repeats threat to bomb Iran's infrastructure if a deal isn't reached, strikes in the Middle East intensify as Trump's deadline looms, Artemis II crew heads home after historic moon mission.
item.content:encoded: Trump repeats threat to bomb Iran's infrastructure if a deal isn't reached, strikes in the Middle East intensify as Trump's deadline looms, Artemis II crew heads home after historic moon mission.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Actress Chase Infiniti talks about new 'Handmaid's Tale' sequel, 'The Testaments'
item.description: NPR's A Martínez speaks with Chase Infiniti, who stars in "The Testaments," based on Margaret Atwood's novel that takes place years after the events told in "The Handmaid's Tale."
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez speaks with Chase Infiniti, who stars in "The Testaments," based on Margaret Atwood's novel that takes place years after the events told in "The Handmaid's Tale."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: How much has the US spent on the war in Iran
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Mark Cancian, a retired Marine colonel and a senior advisor at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, about how much the U.S. has spent on the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Mark Cancian, a retired Marine colonel and a senior advisor at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, about how much the U.S. has spent on the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: The history of the out-of-this-world playlist NASA uses to wake up mission crews
item.description: It's become a tradition: NASA's ground control plays music to wake up the astronauts on a mission. NASA's chief historian Brian Odom shares the history of the practice.
item.content:encoded: It's become a tradition: NASA's ground control plays music to wake up the astronauts on a mission. NASA's chief historian Brian Odom shares the history of the practice.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Michigan wins school's second NCAA basketball title, beating UConn
item.description: Michigan clawed its way to victory Monday, defeating UConn and securing the school's second NCAA men's basketball title. NPR's A Martinez talks to Martenzie Johnson of ESPN and Andscape about the win.
item.content:encoded: Michigan clawed its way to victory Monday, defeating UConn and securing the school's second NCAA men's basketball title. NPR's A Martinez talks to Martenzie Johnson of ESPN and Andscape about the win.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Nearly 50 years later, Cincinnati may get a real 'WKRP'
item.description: Nearly 50 years after a sitcom made the name famous, it appears there will actually be a "WKRP in Cincinnati."
item.content:encoded: Nearly 50 years after a sitcom made the name famous, it appears there will actually be a "WKRP in Cincinnati."
item.dc:creator: Hosts
